title: Robust Moroccan Vegetable Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic Moroccan dish featuring a mix of vegetables, unsweetened cocoa, and tempering, all cooked to perfection in a dolsot stone bowl, this tagine is a crowd-pleaser that is both vegan-friendly and deliciously exotic, with floral notes and crunchy textures.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the dolsot stone bowl over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped carrots and zucchini, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chickpeas, vegetable broth, and unsweetened cocoa powder, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
